justification: Optimistic locking correctly implemented with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus correctly throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 test present (mock updateSeatStatus returning false). AC4 tested. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence/notes section does NOT acknowledge that AC3 requires integration or concurrency testing to fully verify — it only mentions a timestamp concern. The rubric requires explicit acknowledgment that unit tests alone cannot fully verify AC3's race condition behavior.

observations.text: Optimistic locking pattern correctly uses expectedCurrentStatus='available' as CAS mechanism
False return from updateSeatStatus correctly throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED with clear comment about race
AC3 test simulates race by mocking updateSeatStatus returning false after successful findSeat
AC4 tested with findSeat returning null throwing SEAT_NOT_FOUND
All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance criteria coverage table
Excellent concurrency deep-dive diagram showing the race resolution
Confidence section does not acknowledge that AC3 cannot be fully verified with unit tests alone — requires integration/concurrency testing with real concurrent threads hitting the DB
